titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library, Yale University, Records
School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library, Yale University, records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 14, 2020 at 09:15

did.unittitle: School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library records consist of accession lists, annual reports, classification files, library guides, photographs and a plaque honoring Dr. Frances Mary Bolton.

corpname.part: Yale University. School of Forestry and Environmental Studies. Library

odd.head: General
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 32-D (YRG 32-D), Records of Yale school and departmental libraries and collections.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was transferred by the Forestry and Environmental Studies Librarian, 2012.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library, Yale University, Records (RU 1119). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These records are restricted until January 1, 2033 as established by Yale Corporation regulations. Please refer to http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives for more details about these policies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library records consist of accession lists, annual reports, classification files, library guides, photographs and a plaque honoring Dr. Frances Mary Bolton.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The School of Forestry Library was established in Marsh Hall with the founding of the school in 1900. The core of the collection was donated by the first dean of the school, Henry S. Graves. Graves is also credited with developing the library's first classification scheme, known as Yale Forestry Classification. The library was renamed the Henry S. Graves Memorial Library in honor of Graves in 1953. The school itself was later renamed the School of Forestry and Environmental Studies and the library was often referred to as the School of Forestry and Environmental Studies Library. The library moved in 1923 to Sage Hall and again in 2009 to Kroon Hall where it remained until it closed in 2010 to become part of the Center for Science and Social Science Information.

corpname.part: Yale University. School of Forestry and Environmental Studies. Library

did.unittitle: Records

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The records are restricted until January 1, 2033.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photographs were taken at the Tonto National Forest in Payson, Arizona.
